sections.paragraph: m. a ten, a decade-(a) of days (like שָׁבוּעַ a hebdomad, a week), Gen 24:55 also used of the last day of a decade, i.e. the tenth day (of the month), Exo 12:5 Lev 16:29 (compare Gr. δεκάς, ἐννεάς, τετράς, used of the tenth, ninth, or fourth day of a month, and the Eth. ዐሡር, ኀሙስ፡ of the tenth, fifth day, etc., see Lud. Gramm. p. 100).-(b) of strings, chords; hence a decachord, Psa 92:3 fully (by apposit.) נֶבֶל עָשֹׁור the decachord nablium, Psa 33:2 144:9 .
